summary: Good soundboard capture of a 1993 show highlighted by a scorching Terrapin jam and strong Dark Star, though some listeners note shed and mixed set flow.
review: The recording is a DAT‑mastered soundboard tape that sounds generally clear with a bit of bleed (“shed”), delivering a solid listening experience. Musically the night shines on Jerry Garcia’s ripping lead work – especially in Terrapin – and features memorable Dark Star, Way to Go Home and an extra‑cool LSD encore, but a few reviewers felt the first set was short and some songs fell flat.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviews praise the performance (Terrapin, Dark Star) and deem the soundboard capture decent, but a few note recording bleed and disappointing moments, leading to an overall good‑to‑average rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Recorded from a DAT master using AMS ST‑250 cardioid/blumlein mics; reviewers mention some shed/bleed and occasional muffling, but overall the mix is clear and enjoyable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as short and a bit melancholy; second set flows better with strong jams, though some listeners felt the Dark Star never fully took off.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is repeatedly praised for shredding, blistering licks, and ripping lead work throughout the night.
